description: For every JSON database, every SQL database that's permutation-equivalent to it, and every supported jq query, evaluating the jq query and evaluating its translated SQL query produce equivalent results.
name: query_equiv
title: Headline equivalence theorem
type:
∀ (jd : JDB) (sd : SDB) (jq : JQuery),
  permEquiv jd sd → result_equiv (eval_jquery jd jq) (eval_squery sd (jquery_to_squery jq))

description: If two databases are permutation-equivalent (multiset equal) then they are also set-equivalent. Used as a stepping stone in the find/drop arms of query_equiv.
name: permEquiv_implies_equiv
title: Permutation equivalence implies set equivalence
type: ∀ {jd : JDB} {sd : SDB}, permEquiv jd sd → equiv jd sd

description: Two databases are equivalent iff every JSON record is in the column-form rows and vice-versa.
name: db_equiv_bridge
title: JDB ↔ SDB structural bridge
type:
∀ (jd : JDB) (sd : SDB),
  Iff (equiv jd sd)
    (And
      (∀ (u : Juser),
        Iff (List.instMembership.mem jd u) (List.instMembership.mem sd.toRows (toS u)))
      (∀ (s : Suser),
        Iff (List.instMembership.mem sd.toRows s) (List.instMembership.mem jd (toJ s))))

description: Evaluating a Cond on the JSON record agrees with evaluating it on the SQL row produced by `toS`.
name: eval_bridgeJ
title: Cond.evalJ bridges through toS

description: Applying a conditional UPDATE to a JSON record and converting to SQL produces the same row as applying the SQL UPDATE to the converted row.
name: applyUpdateIf_bridge
title: Per-row UPDATE preserves the JSON ↔ SQL bridge
type:
∀ (col : Col) (v : Value) (c : Cond) (u : Juser),
  Eq (apply_update_ifS col v c (toS u)) (toS (apply_update_ifJ col v c u))

description: Converting a JSON user to a SQL row and back yields the same record.
name: toJ_toS
title: toS ∘ toJ is identity (round-trip lemma 1)

description: Converting a SQL row to a JSON user and back yields the same record.
name: toS_toJ
title: toJ ∘ toS is identity (round-trip lemma 2)
